sentence: Subjective:
HPI: Mr. Thompson is a 67-year-old male with a history of essential hypertension, hyperlipidemia, and osteoarthritis, who presents with fluctuating and elevated blood pressures.
He reports feeling lightheaded and experiencing slight headaches, with occasional upper blood pressure readings over 180.
He denies fever, nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, palpitations, and passing out.
He has a history of being able to manage his blood pressure well for the past three to four weeks but has experienced periods of lightheadedness and high blood pressure readings.
He also mentions a slight heaviness in his chest and some unsteadiness when experiencing dizziness.
He takes lisinopril 20mg twice daily for hypertension and atorvastatin for hyperlipidemia.
Past medical history: Mr. Thompson has a history of essential hypertension, hyperlipidemia, and osteoarthritis.
Review of systems: He reports mild bibasilar rales, trace lower extremity edema, and a grade three systolic ejection murmur.
Current medications:
Lisinopril 20mg twice daily for hypertension
Atorvastatin for hyperlipidemia
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]

Physical exam: The physical exam is mostly normal and unremarkable, with a grade three out of six systolic ejection murmur, mild bibasilar rales, and trace lower extremity edema on the extremity exam.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: Mr. Thompson presents with elevated and fluctuating blood pressures, which require adjustments to his current medication regimen.
Problem list: Hypertension confirmed; Hyperlipidemia confirmed; Osteoarthritis stable.
Plan:
I increased Mr. Thompson's lisinopril to 60mg twice daily and added 25mg of hydrochlorothiazide daily to help control his blood pressure.
I also instructed him to check his blood pressure twice a day for the next two weeks.
I will send a cholesterol panel, CBC with differential, comprehensive metabolic panel, and urinalysis to assess his kidney function.

I advised him to continue taking atorvastatin for hyperlipidemia and to continue with his current management plan for osteoarthritis, including taking Tylenol for mild pain and modifying his activity as needed.
Follow-up: I scheduled a follow-up appointment with Mr. Thompson in two weeks to reassess his blood pressure and adjust his medication regimen as needed.
